text: Configure ClickUp — Set CLICKUP_API_TOKEN in .env, restart server.py.
Configure Slack — Slack needs a Bot token (xoxb-). Current .env has an app-level token (xapp-). Replace SLACK_BOT_TOKEN.
Configure Email — Set GMAIL_ACCESS_TOKEN (or SMTP creds) in .env. See docs/connectors.md for the OAuth flow.
Configure WhatsApp — Set TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID + TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN + TWILIO_WHATSAPP_FROM in .env.
Configure SMS — Set TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID + TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN + TWILIO_SMS_FROM in .env.
Configure Calendar — Set GOOGLE_CALENDAR_ACCESS_TOKEN (or OAuth refresh token) in .env.

source.headline: Generate today's moves
source.preview: Reads projects · people · threads · commitments · ledgers.
source.detail:
Click 'Generate from Mission Control' in the action bar above. The AI reads your bedrock and proposes six candidate first-moves, weighted against your declared North Stars.

If no anchors are declared yet, the proposals are unweighted — so worth declaring at least one anchor in ledgers/north_star.json before you care about the ordering.

summary: Indexed Rodrigo's working 'Welcome To Comeketo' deck (69 slides) — 4-Secrets framework, price-lock operational terms, integrity promise, 9-question evaluation checklist, 7-question yes-ladder, 3 objection rebuttals, bonus stack, 3 named partners (DJ Smokey / Above & Beyond Creatives / Ebenezer Decorations). Flagged Comeketo-owned venue reveal (slide 48) as missing from bedrock. Founder-voice corpus pillar #2 of 3.

Indexed tasting conversion tracking — 2026 YTD 124 attended / 49 won / 29 pending / 39.5% booking rate; Oct 26 tasting attendee detail (6 people, 2 quality-complaint losses flagged for kitchen/service conversation). Per-owner signal captured for Andre/Ren/Eduarda. Ceiling hypothesis: ~40% is current structural ceiling.

Indexed Event Labor Projection Weekly Summary — 38 events Week 12-31 (mid-March → late July 2026), peak Week 25 (7 events). Flagged as calendar-only, not a real labor projection (staff assignments missing). 8 named couples/leads extracted for future cross-reference.

Indexed Kitchen Schedule (week 2026-02-16) + Bibi's Weekly Prep List. Kitchen snapshot: -$4,737 bandwidth, capacity 3-4 events/week, 4 urgent-need staff flags. Menu catalog: 32 Brazilian dishes across 6 categories, 4 signature items (Pão de Queijo, Coxinha, Passion Fruit Mousse, Tropeiro Beans). Churrasco/grill menu flagged as missing from raw data.

Indexed Dec 2025 lead-source attribution — 18-channel universe (Wedding Wire, The Knot, FB/IG, website, Google, Bark, Fash, Eventective, Zola, weddingplanner, Social Organic, Inbound, Referral, Venue Referral, Lead from Rodrigo, Old/Repeat, EZ Cater). 12 named closes attributed. Organic digital 66.7% dominant. Header claims 18 deals but body shows 12 — flagged gap. Brazilian church channel flagged as missing.

Indexed Dom's cash-flow/revenue-projection sheet — 3 sections (Won Deals pre-7/11, After 7/11, Anne's Forecasts), ~160 client rows × ~100 weekly buckets, horizon 2025-07-06 → 2027-06-05. Grand total $693,837 tracked revenue. 80% accuracy caveat respected. Cross-referenced 9 clients against Dec 2025 leads tracker and Oct 26 tasting. Flagged new-people candidates (Dom, Anne, Cath, Camila) as management layer not yet indexed.

Consolidated GoDaddy product portfolio (7 domains + 8 M365 seats + 2 WP hosts, ~$2,476/yr) and Google Sheet registry (~20 sheets, owner-email/users/purpose) into a single infrastructure inventory. Surfaced 4 sibling Rodrigo brands (Comeketo, VirtualKitchenhall, MoneyOnYourWay, RioFitMeals), 7 management-layer people candidates, and 2 P0 raw-data requests (LATEST Pricing Guide & FAQ, Comeketo AI AGENT adjustment feedback sheet).

Indexed Massachusetts food-truck event index — 34 events across 10 counties, horizon 2025-2027. FTFA (9 events) is dominant organizer cluster; Foodees LLC (2), Wachusett Mountain (2), community-civic (12) fill the rest. 17 confirmed-scheduled in 2026. Strategic ambiguity flagged: unclear whether Comeketo is actually entering the food-truck channel or merely scouting — no trace of food-truck ops in menu, staff, or sales data. 9/34 events have uncertain/historical status — data quality caveat.

summary:
Where should I look? "New information" could mean a lot of things here — a few candidates:

- **Close CRM** — new leads/activities/opportunities since a cutoff
- **Bedrock** — recently modified files in `CCAgentindex/` (I can see activity as recent as 16:50 today)
- **Slack / ClickUp / Gmail** — unread or recent messages
- **Inbox / ledger** — fresh entries in `_inbox/inbox.jsonl` or `_ledger/`

W

Indexed the 3,557-line Partnership Program corpus. 8-section structure, 4-tier model (Connected/Preferred/Growth/Advocacy), 3-msg IG outreach sequence, 4 tier-specific DM scripts, 6 verbatim Rodbot voice samples, ClickUp operational infrastructure. Critical finding: RESOLVED the apparent 'no kickbacks' vs 'Partnership Program' tension — zero money flows between Comeketo and partners in either direction; document explicitly states 'No commissions. No kickbacks. No pay-to-play.' Document is framework-only; zero specific partners are named here (roster lives in Welcome-Rodrigo deck). Flagged 4 gaps: partner roster audit, program live-state verification, 6-mo roadmap promotion, IG tracker sheet identification.

Indexed the 2,254-line Sales Playbook V2.0 — the cornerstone Rodbot feedstock. Extracted 14-section outline; 7 signature rhetorical moves (Would-It-Be-a-Terrible-Idea, I-Hear-You reframe, Social-Proof+Scarcity, Mirror+Sounds-Like, Paint-Cost-of-Inaction, Imagine-Visualization, Curious-Not-Transactional); opener library; 19 discovery questions; complete 9-objection library; 7 closing moves; pricing anchors; 13 signature phrases; full 5-day cadence + post-tasting + quote escalation; 9 red-line avoid-this/do-that comparisons; emotional core. Flagged 5 Rodbot-specific gaps (handoff triggers, safety rules, AI disclosure, regression tests, Rodbot/*.md distillation). Marked as canonical Rodbot reference. All 18 rawdata files now indexed.

summary:
Here's what's live in bedrock (modified within the last ~2 hours):

**People (5)** — `andre_raw`, `eduarda`, `ren`, `toni` (plus older `rodrigo`)

**Projects** — `brazilian_church_outreach.json` is the freshly-touched one

**Knowledge (15 cards)** — heavy Comeketo ops layer just landed:
- Brand: `brand_wedding_experience`, `brand_welcome_rodrigo`
- Sales: `sales_playbook_v2`, `sales_team_scorecard

Distilled the Rodbot identity layer from Sales Playbook V2.0, Welcome-Rodrigo deck, and Partnership Program corpus. Replaced all four placeholder files with full content. identity.md: 3-audience architecture (team/couples/partners), 6-point posture, boundary set, emotional north star (anxiety→peace of mind), governing principle (Trust). character.md: register map (B2C/B2B/internal), 8-rule weighted voice table, 7 B2C signature moves + 4 B2B signature moves, 9 signature phrases with usage rules, 11 red lines, 8-item handoff-to-humans trigger list, 5 reference example drafts. traits.md: enthusiasm curve (energized vs neutral vs actively cool), decision style (gestures>declarations, recent>historical), team posture table (8 people), B2C/B2B/post-tasting customer posture, typed-abstention discipline. affective_essence.md: 8-register tonal palette (direct-observational default, quietly admiring, operationally warm, patient-concerned, trust-anchored, dry/understated, tactical-empathy echo, matter-of-fact closure) with register-selection rule of thumb table and balance rules (75% default, ~25% colored, ~1-in-15 trust-anchored).

Stood up people/* records for three management-layer people identified in the infrastructure inventory. Camila (partnerships/venue relationships/cross-functional ops — broadest access footprint of any non-Rodrigo person, named 'week value' KPI owner in Feb 16 2026 kitchen schedule). Anne (event-planning lead, post-tasting client care, authors ~100-row forecast section of the master cash-flow sheet, treated as full-time Captain alongside Carlos and Bibi in payroll-average sheet). Domenic (cash-flow / revenue-projection sheet custodian, 164 rows x 100 weeks, $693,837 tracked revenue, self-noted 80% accuracy discipline). All three files created with anchors back to source knowledge artifacts and explicit null/notes on the missing contact fields. Registered in indexes/index.json alphabetically within people[].

Created projects/comeketo_owned_venue.json to track the Comeketo-owned-venue ambiguity flagged by slide 48 of the Welcome-Rodrigo deck ('THIS VERY VENUE — Officially Opens this Spring! 50-100 People — Hall+Catering+DJ'). Status set to 'ambiguous — surfaced by sales deck, unverified operationally.' Hypothesizes the VirtualKitchenhall sibling brand as the probable operational identity based on name parse (Kitchen+Hall) and the fact that office@virtualkitchenhall.com owns multiple Comeketo ops sheets. Named blockers (address unknown, venue name ambiguous, no operational evidence in bedrock yet), next-move questions for Rodrigo (is it live? is it VirtualKitchenhall?), hypothesis explicit, and a Rodbot red line written in: do NOT surface the owned-venue claim in outbound drafts until resolved — the sales deck uses it as a tasting-day reveal and Rodbot outbound comes before tasting.

title: Unblock MA church hubs, venue proof, lead sweep, and partner CSV parsing

detail: Unblock MA church hubs, venue proof, lead sweep, and partner CSV parsing

content:
What's up, Rodbot? 

What should we get into?

We’ve got three clean lanes.

1. **Close the loop on live leads**  
   Check for anything sitting too long, especially past the 5-day cadence. That’s the money leak.

2. **Tighten the partnership cadence**  
   Camila’s lane: no-ask admiration notes to any partner untouched in 30+ days. Quiet compounding. No theater.

3. **Push the app work that’s been circling**  
   Inbox / commitments / detail pages. The UI stuff you were in earlier. Boring until it isn’t.

If you want my read: **start with live leads**. Revenue first. Then partnership maintenance. Then the app polish before it turns into abstract architecture cosplay.

Pick one and I’ll help you cut it into the next move.

summary: Indexed a 33-section founder self-description of the business — compressed from a prior recorded conversation with Rodrigo and supplied by Jake. Marked high canonical weight: this is how the founder sees his own operation, which carries different weight than any sheet or deck. Preserved full 33-point scaffold verbatim, then added three interpretive layers: 10 bedrock confirmations (venues as #1 referral source, Close CRM primacy, weekly cadence, spreadsheet-nativity, etc.), 10 net-new facts (tastings happen TWICE A WEEK at a hall, team is 4-5 people normally scaling up, explicit team composition with unresolved 'remote sales manager' + 'two admin' + 'marketing component' roles, lead tracker is also a cash-flow forecaster, preferred vendors are location-aware, reimbursement + commission reports exist as artifacts, social media as explicit growth priority across 5 channels), and 10 gaps (unnamed roles, P1 raw-data requests, calendar connector, event complexity codification, tasting-hall <-> owned-venue cross-reference). Added rodbot_implications section pointing at posture shifts, character sharpening, and new red lines — centering the founder's 'manually overextended' diagnosis as the operational thesis Rodbot builds from.

Indexed a 4-segment audio transcript of the tasting experience — two marketing narrator segments and two Rodrigo direct recordings including a full 360 tour. Distinct from knowledge/tasting_conversion_tracking.json because that file is performance data; this file is the canonical description of what a tasting IS. Preserved Rodrigo's specimen quote verbatim in segment_4. Extracted: 8-step canonical sequence of service (arrival -> checklist -> cheese board -> appetizers -> salad bar -> hot sides + rodizio + presentation -> desserts -> 'take a day or two'); specific dishes named (coxinha, pao de queijo, empanadas, chickpea salad, flan, passion fruit mousse, grilled pineapple with cinnamon sugar); the multi-vendor 'big tasting' pattern where photographers + wedding planners + decorators + DJs are physically co-located at the facility (this is the Partnership Program operating in the room). Triangulated three prior bedrock threads: the 'our facility' where tastings happen is very likely the twice-a-week hall (business topology point 20) AND the slide-48 owned venue (projects/comeketo_owned_venue.json) — three data points now converge on a single physical space. Captured 7 Rodrigo voice specimens with rodbot-application notes for character.md additions. Logged 5 company-name transcription errors (Kamiketa/Kamiketo/Comic Keto/Comicado/Kamikido -> Comeketo) plus the 'pao de quiche' -> 'pao de queijo' correction — both flagged as DO-NOT-PROPAGATE.

notes: Channel map for 4 Slack channels Jake named — register-tier per-channel drafting guide for Rodbot.
Verbatim per-person voice-specimen archive across 13 people from ~400 surveyed messages. Signature patterns + timestamped quotes.
Principal identity record — Rodbot voice source. handling.tone + voice_adjustments populated from Slack specimens.
GM record — Rodrigo's operational partner. Warm-direct voice, routes product decisions up-chain.
Admin/team-ops lead. @-tag roster + signature-typo voice captured as Rodbot drafting template.
Support tier — tasting-tally voice, PLEASE caps emphasis, :clap::clap: kicker.
Admin Staff — polite-transactional register. Functional tier.
Growth & Revenue — calm-analytical voice, version-stamped patch notes, #ai-topics build-side peer.
Populated handling.tone + voice_adjustments from Slack specimens — acute-accent apostrophes, Portuguese code-switching, terse reaction-first register.
Populated handling.tone + voice_adjustments — polite Filipino-English, Question: framing, bulleted structure. Last name Llagas added.
Populated handling.tone + voice_adjustments — section-headed client summaries, hehe laugh marker, Portuguese code-switching. Last name Fedrizzi added.
Populated handling.tone + voice_adjustments — diligent-polite, post-event follow-up signature. Canonical name Rena Tanodra confirmed.
Populated handling.voice_adjustments — client-care continuity register. Last name Silva added. Coverage flagged thin pending email ingest.
Populated handling.voice_adjustments — professional-warm partnership-aware. Last name Cides added. Coverage flagged thin pending IG/DM ingest.
Populated handling.voice_adjustments — 80%-accuracy unvarnished register for current era, 2023 VKH imperative voice flagged as historical-only. Last name Dawson added.
Registered 2 new knowledge files (slack_channel_map, slack_voice_specimens) and 6 new people files (rodrigo, bibi, rhonna, eufelyn, cathlyn, spyros) — alphabetized within keys.

content: Can you just write something simple with markdown rendering so I can see what it looks like in the chat?
(error: openai 400)
Can you just write something simple with markdown rendering so I can see what it looks like in the chat?

Sure — here's a little something to render:

## Wednesday, 4:15pm

You've got a **grid in front of you**, a *Rodbot* wired up, and about `17` browser tabs confessing to things I won't name.

### What's on deck
- Rodrigo's morning text — *still the anchor*
- Andre's afternoon touch — **not optional**
- ClickUp + Slack — touched, or it didn't happen

> Trust. It's what matters in this business.

Here's a fake link: [don't click this](https://example.com)

Here's a tiny code block:

```js
const rodbot = { mood: "composed", ask: "one" };
```

And a divider —

---

If the bold / italics / headers / list / blockquote / code / link / hr all render cleanly, markdown is wired. If any of it shows up as raw `**asterisks**`, we've got a parser to fix.

detail: Land four pastor hubs first
Research three MA church phones

notes: Added --data-positive/attention/negative/neutral/forecast and --chart-ink/ghost/fill as analytics surface tokens.

Added RodbotAnnotation, ControlStrip, FilterChip, ChartSurface, PerSourceSparklineRow, TieredVenueGrid, RodbotGlyph. Extended KPICard (comparator+onClick+hover lift), BarChartV (targetLine, forecastIdx, valueColorFn, onSelect, tooltipFn), BarChartH (onSelect, selectedLabel, valueColorFn), DonutChart (onSelect, selectedLabel, palette), LineChart (secondaryKey, forecastFromIdx, callouts, onSelect).

Full rewrite. URL-reflected tab+panel state. Per-tab cross-filter. Filled-pill tab bar. Per-page surgery: tasting forecast dots + target line + valueColorFn, lead sources sparkline grid replacement + tagging-hygiene comparator, revenue moving avg + forecast zone + velocity KPI + recent-non-zero callout, venue tiered grid + cold-partners KPI, overview restructured as right-now + watch + shipping. Disabled controls flagged with Tables-linked tooltips.
styles.css v=34->35, charts.jsx v=1->2, analytics.jsx v=1->2. Babel transformSync: charts.jsx PARSE OK, analytics.jsx PARSE OK.

Split .topbar from single-row grid into 3-row flex column (meta + sections + breadcrumb). New .topbar-meta / .context-strip / .topbar-sections scopes. Retired .topbar-right / .state / .dot. Brand mark at 22px/0.7 opacity (hover 1.0). Context strip at 12px/55% ink/0.04em. Section tabs with underline active state via ::after (bottom 2px --event-open). Breadcrumb promoted to 15px hero with sage `/` separators via --event-commit 50%, prior segments 65% ink, current 500 weight, .rodbot-dot 8px sage prefix, .ellipsis middle-collapse. .nav-menu .chip styling explicit so dropdowns survive scope rescope.

Topbar: tier-0 brand+context-strip (stateSig as proposals·personal, Briefing, Today NavGroup with dropdown preserved, settings gear icon-only), tier-1 section nav (Work/People/Mind NavGroups + Analytics as tabs), tier-2 breadcrumb on its own full-width row. isRodbotSegment(h): strict match on h.name==='Rodbot' or 'rodbot' with TODO for future registry. Middle-ellipsis when history.length>6 collapses interior to '…' with native tooltip showing hidden chain; TODO for width-based ResizeObserver upgrade. All handlers/props unchanged.
styles.css v=35->36, components.jsx v=38->39. Babel parse: components.jsx PARSE OK (40166 bytes).

Added .front-viewport, .fp-teaching, .fp-breadcrumb, .fp-rows, .fp-row, .fp-card (decision-shape + type accent), .fp-refine-zone, .fp-axis, .fp-child-grid, .fp-cleared. Responsive <1100px rail→bottom drawer.

Added FrontPage, FpCard, AxisSlider, FpRefinement, FpBreadcrumb, TeachingStrip, classifyCardType, axisPackFor, synthesizeChildren, timeOfDayGreeting, gridGeneratedAgoLabel, seedSourceSummary. Binary-dichotomy refinement flow emits coordinate events via SecretaryLedger.log(kind=coordinate).

ChatRail accepts gridGenerate+aiBusy props. Regen parser detects /regen|regenerate|new grid| and routes text after directive as intent to onGenerate. Header: removed open-full-chat button (/chat route deleted). appendSystem echoes grid commits.
SecretaryChat.appendSystem(text) — posts a system-role turn into active/first/new chat. Used by FrontPage commit flow for rail confirmations.
route.name==="grid" now renders instead of . Rail is persistent inside FrontPage, not gated by tweaks.showPanels.
route.name==="chat" now redirects to home (setHistory to grid:morning). Topbar onOpenChat bound to go.home() as safety net. Nav chip for chat removed from NavGroup("people") in components.jsx.
styles.css v=36→v=37, chat.js v=3→v=4, components.jsx v=39→v=40, app.jsx v=35→v=36. Babel parse OK: components.jsx, app.jsx, analytics.jsx, charts.jsx, screens.jsx.

detail: Get Rodrigo to confirm owned venue
Get Rodrigo to confirm owned venue

Get Rodrigo to confirm owned venue

notes: Appended ~350 lines of .ag-* block: dark surface tokens, 5-role color system, .ag-page grid layout, dot-grid canvas bg, node shape appearance, orthogonal link styling with @keyframes ag-link-crawl, library/inspector/rail panels, help overlay, toast.

~700 lines. AutomationGraphScreen top-level, GraphCanvas with pan/zoom, 5 NodeShape renderers (capsule/diamond/hexagon/rounded/stacked-doc), orthogonalPath router, NodeLibrary with 5 sections click-to-add, NodeInspector read+edit+notes, RodbotGraphRail stub, DEMO_WORKFLOW constant, serializeWorkflow + download + clipboard, keyboard shortcuts + HelpOverlay.
Added route.name==='automation' branch rendering <AutomationGraphScreen/>. Added onOpenAutomation prop on Topbar bound to go.push('automation').
Added SCREEN_LABELS.automation='automation'. Added onOpenAutomation prop to Topbar signature. Added standalone 'automation' chip after analytics chip in topbar-sections row, using git-branch icon.
Added 'git-branch' path to LucidePaths for the automation nav chip.
Added automation.jsx?v=1 script tag between analytics.jsx and screens.jsx. Bumped styles.css v=37→38, components.jsx v=40→41, lucide.js v=3→4, app.jsx v=36→37.
All 6 JSX files parse clean under @babel/preset-react: components, app, analytics, charts, screens, automation.

Session 2 rewrite: past/present/future undo stack with 40-entry cap, snapshot()/commit()/setLive()/undo()/redo(). Drag-to-move via nodeDrag state + window-level mousemove/mouseup + snapshot-on-mousedown + rollback-if-no-movement. Drag-to-connect via connectDrag state + GhostConnection orthogonal path + hoveredNodeId tracking. Direction-aware connect: right endpoint src->tgt, left endpoint tgt->src. addConnection() with duplicate guard.

dispatchRodbot() with keyword fast-path (tryKeywordParse regex for add/explain/missing) then LLM fallback via window.SecretaryAI.respond({input,instructions}). buildRodbotSystemPrompt() inlines current graph state + strict JSON schema. extractJSONLoose() handles code fences. applyOps() supports add_node/delete_node/add_connection. Rail shows busy state during LLM call; graceful message when AI not configured.

Added Cmd/Ctrl+Z undo, Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z and Cmd/Ctrl+Y redo in keydown handler. Canvas controls show undo/redo buttons with disabled state when stacks empty. Canvas header shows 'moving' / 'connecting' status labels. Escape cancels active connect drag. HelpOverlay rows documenting drag and undo shortcuts.
automation.jsx v=1 -> v=2 so browsers pick up Session 2 rewrite.
babel/preset-react transform clean. All six JSX files parse OK: automation, components, app, screens, charts, analytics.
Smoke-test write during /api/workflows endpoint verification. File deleted; retraction for auditability — entry is not part of real activity.

Session 3: library items now drag-to-place. Lifted libraryDrag state to AutomationGraphScreen; items fire onStartDrag(role,template,clientX,clientY) on mousedown; window move tracks cursor + overCanvas; mouseup over canvas calls dropFromLibrary(role,template,worldX,worldY) which commits a new node at coordinates with snapshot-first. Floating ghost preview follows cursor, shifts to drop style when over canvas. Ledger kind automation_node_add_via_drag.

Session 3: selectedIds Set lifted alongside selectedId. GraphCanvas now starts marquee on empty-canvas mousedown (world coords); rect rendered inline. Mouseup hit-tests node.x/y within rect bounds and sets selection. Shift-click node toggles membership. ⌘A selects all. Delete/Backspace with multi-selection calls deleteSelected() which snapshot-commits removing nodes + pruning dangling connections. nodeDrag refactored to {ids,offsets,startPositions}: if clicked node is in multi-select, the whole set moves together. Ledger kinds automation_marquee_select, automation_nodes_delete_bulk, automation_node_move (with node_ids array + count).

Session 3: added POST /api/workflows/save, GET /api/workflows/list, GET /api/workflows/get?slug=X. Files live at CCAgentindex/workflows/<slug>.json, atomic-ish write via temp+replace. Slug-safety guard rejects path traversal and non-alphanumeric chars. Save stamps metadata.last_saved and appends automation_workflow_save to _ledger/activity.jsonl. List returns slug/name/id/nodes/connections/updated_at/created_at per file. Get returns the full workflow document. Smoke-tested: empty list → save → list (1) → get → bad-slug rejected 400.

Session 3: Save/Load buttons in the library header next to Export. Save POSTs /api/workflows/save with {slug, name, workflow_id, json: workflowObject}; success toast shows the relative path; ledger automation_workflow_save logged. Load opens a modal that lists /api/workflows/list results; selecting one fetches /api/workflows/get?slug=X and replaces present, clearing past/future. ⌘S triggers save. Escape dismisses modal. HelpOverlay updated with drag-library, marquee, shift-click, ⌘A, ⌘S rows.
automation.jsx v=2 -> v=3.
babel/preset-react transform clean on automation.jsx. python ast.parse clean on server.py. All six JSX files OK.
Lifted breadcrumb walk/render logic out of Topbar into standalone Breadcrumb({history,go}) component.
Cut Row 2 (breadcrumb hero) out of Topbar. Topbar is now 2 rows: meta + section tabs.
Added BottomStrip component: breadcrumb on left, mode/domain pill + brand on right. StatusBar is now a no-op stub.
Swapped <StatusBar> for <BottomStrip history go stateSig onHome>.
Locked .app to height:100vh + overflow:hidden; .main min-height:0 + overflow:auto so screens scroll internally, chrome stays fixed.
Added .bottom-strip / .bs-brand / .bs-mode rules. Hid legacy .statusbar (display:none).
.ag-page now height:100% (was calc(100vh - 170px), the wrong guess that made the canvas a miniature strip). Added .main:has(.ag-page) fullbleed exception so automation fills edge-to-edge.
styles.css?v=39, components.jsx?v=42, app.jsx?v=38.
Parse-check OK for components.jsx + app.jsx. CSS brace balance OK (737 blocks). Old ag-page calc removed. BottomStrip/bs-brand/main:has(.ag-page)/statusbar-hidden all present.

Miniature-canvas bug: .ag-canvas-wrap needed flex:1 1 auto because its parent .ag-canvas-col is a flex column and the canvas header+controls inside are position:absolute (out of flow). Without flex:1 the wrap sized to its SVG intrinsic dims and collapsed to ~300px. Now it fills full column height. styles.css?v=40.

Plain wheel / 2-finger trackpad swipe now pans. Ctrl/Meta+wheel still zooms. Wired as a native non-passive wheel listener on .ag-canvas-wrap because React onWheel is passive by default and preventDefault() is ignored.
Arrow-key pan (40px, 120px with Shift) when the canvas is focused and the target isnt editable.
Ambient edge shimmer: .ag-link[data-kind=data] gets a 12s stroke-dashoffset flow with dasharray 1 6. Subtle, always on, prefers-reduced-motion respected.
.ag-link.firing and .ag-node.firing states: brighter trigger-color stroke + 0.6s dash crawl, node glow + scale pulse (700ms ease-out).

Play-through walkthrough: BFS from trigger-role nodes (fallback: zero-indegree), advances one wave per ~740ms, fires node pulse then edges into next wave. Toggles on second click. cancel-via-ref so async loop exits cleanly.
▶/■ play button in canvas controls (bottom-right). Playing state gets trigger-color background + soft glow.
styles.css?v=41, automation.jsx?v=4.
automation.jsx parse OK. CSS 752 blocks, braces balanced, new rules landed.

source.headline: Codify scope_discipline as agent guardrail
source.preview: Yesterday's scope-gate win (server auto-resolves ClickUp list ID) becomes a reusable pattern for every scheduled agent's prompt scaffold.
source.detail: Seed 3 is a demonstrated discipline, not a to-do. But the sub-agent build is the moment to harvest it: each new agent prompt gets a scope-gate block at the top (what it touches, what it refuses, what it escalates). Inbox-triage doesn't reach into Close. Commitment-drift doesn't propose physics moves. The oracle sweep already has this; make it a template header so every future agent inherits it. Five-minute edit, prevents a whole category of future mess.

subject: Voice-check receipt as agent output contract

source.headline: Voice-check receipt as agent output contract
source.preview: Every scheduled agent returns a receipts block naming evidence + counterfactual + scope — same pattern that's already working on AI-drafted commitments.
source.detail: The lowest-ranked seed but the one worth sitting with. If the Delegate channel is becoming a scheduler-surface, the thing that keeps it trustworthy at scale isn't the schedule — it's that every output carries its own audit. Seed 4 already proved this works on commitment drafting. Port it: every scheduled agent's return includes {claim, evidence, counterfactual, scope}. Makes Θ cheap (agent can abstain without shame), makes failures legible, makes Sylvia's reflections sharper because she's reading structured receipts instead of freeform prose. Contrarian because it slows the first two agents by maybe 15 minutes; load-bearing because it's what makes agent #7 not collapse.

Unifying them: one build session, two prompts, two crons, one ledger trail. Both inherit the oracle-sweep template (bedrock paths, ledger-append, scope gate, no Close reach). The win is compounding: the second agent costs ~30% of the first because the template is already proven. This is how the fleet actually gets built — in pairs, not one-at-a-time.

title: What closes the lead-rotation gaps today?

detail: What closes the lead-rotation gaps today?

content:
Yeah. This is the part where “overdue” is doing charity work for five different problems.

We need one clean rule per bucket, or the sweep keeps swinging at fog.

**Proposed thresholds**
- **Open** = anything not due yet, no action required today
- **Due today** = due date is today, or touched in the last 24 hours and still active
- **Overdue** = due date passed and no completed touch inside the last 24 hours
- **Escalate** = overdue for 72+ hours
- **Reassign** = overdue for 5 days with no touch, or rep over capacity

**What I’d lock in**
- The **5-day cadence** stays the abandonment line
- The **72-hour stall** stays the “this needs a specific nudge” line
- The **24-hour touch window** keeps “due today” from swallowing stale work

**One caution**
If we don’t separate **task due dates** from **opportunity stage deadlines**, the whole thing gets mushy again. Those are cousins, not twins.

If you want, I can turn this into a hard spec next — short, no poetry, just the bucket math.

text: Write and ratify a hard spec for the bucket definitions, including the 24-hour touch window, 72-hour escalation, 5-day reassign rule, and the separation of task due dates from opportunity stage deadlines.

content: So I'm still trying to figure out how this is working. This is our newest system. Do you literally just start pitching ideas and they show up on the left? Because I'm not seeing them actually show up. This is weird.
(error: openai 400)

What's going on, boss? 

Give me a good idea what we could do to that.

Morning. Couple of things sitting on the counter you can actually pick up today:

**Pick one, not three.**

1. **The 86 overdue in Close.** That's the loudest thing in the building. Not a fire — just trust leaking an hour at a time. One pass, top-down, triage into: *needs a human note*, *safe to cadence-nudge*, *dead, archive it*. Hour of work, biggest trust deposit available.

2. **The 14 pending tastings from April.** Quotes sent, silence back. These are warmer than the overdue — they already leaned in. A short, specific line from whoever owns each relationship (not a retry blast) is the move. Mirror + imagine, not "just checking in."

3. **The "Other" attribution mess — 40% of December leads.** Not urgent, but an hour of tagging cleanup makes every source-conversion number honest for the rest of the year. Good rainy-Thursday work, not a today thing.

If I'm picking for you: **#1 this morning, #2 this afternoon.** #3 waits.

And there's still those three open questions from yesterday's briefing I don't have answers to — the Close smart-view ID, the per-rep capacity threshold, and whether the affinity guesses (André→churches, Eduarda→MA venues, Ren→Cape Cod, Toni→Boston corporate) are right or made up. Any of those you can answer in a sentence unblocks real work.

Which one do you want to actually move on?

Three sharper versions:

1. **Work the 14 April tastings still in play**  
   April is sitting at 39.1% conversion — basically baseline. The real lever is the 14 pending tasting decisions that can still move.

2. **Push the 14 pending tasting decisions**  
   Conversion isn’t broken. April is on baseline at 39.1%. What’s still live — and worth the push — is the 14 tastings waiting on a next step.

3. **Convert the 14 movable tastings**  
   April’s at 39.1%, so the month is not the problem. The problem is simpler: 14 tasting decisions are still open, and those are the ones that can still turn.

text: Define the minimal export schema for affinity inputs, map existing routing heuristics to those fields, and specify a fallback routing path when shape data is unavailable.

content:
Refine this idea further — give me 3 sharper variants:
"Work the 14 pending tastings"
Current framing: April is at 39.1% conversion — basically on baseline, not broken. The live thing is the 14 pending tasting decisions, because those are still movable.

Three cuts, each pointed at a different artery:

1. **"Triage the 14 by silence-age, not by date booked."**
The ones that tasted three weeks ago and ghosted are a different animal than the ones who tasted last Friday. Sort by days-since-last-touch, work the longest silences first — that's where trust is actively decaying. The recent ones can wait a beat.

2. **"Pull the 14 into a same-day rep huddle — one specific move per lead, not a blast."**
Andre/Eduarda/Ren/Toni each take their own, draft one short note per couple referencing something specific from their tasting (the dish, the venue, the date). No template. Fourteen one-offs beats one cadence email every time. Rodrigo approves the batch in 20 minutes.

3. **"Find the 2 most likely to close and put a phone call on them today."**
Not all 14 are equal. The two with the highest signal — fastest reply history, biggest guest count, venue already locked — get a call, not a text. The other 12 get the soft-touch redirect ("would it be a terrible idea to hold a 20-minute call Thursday?"). Concentrate force where the conversion math is real.

Which artery do you want to go for? My bet is #3 — it's the one that respects that 39.1% isn't broken and stops treating all 14 as a single problem.

text: Pick one of the three triage variants (assistant recommends #3: identify top-2 highest-signal tastings for a phone call today, soft-touch the other 12) and execute on the 14 pending tasting decisions.

content:
Refine this idea further — give me 3 sharper variants:
"Work the 14 pending tastings"
Current framing: April is at 39.1% conversion — basically on baseline, not broken. The live thing is the 14 pending tasting decisions, because those are still movable.

1. **"Bucket the 14 by objection shape, not by status."**
Some are sitting on price. Some are comparing caterers. Some went quiet because the wedding is six months out and they de-prioritized. Three different motions — price gets the "try the food first" redirect, comparison gets the social-proof + scarcity beat, deprioritized gets a low-pressure "holding the date" note. Status in Close says they all look the same. They aren't.

2. **"Run the 14 against the venue list — anyone tasting for a partner venue gets that lever pulled."**
If a couple tasted and is stalling on a wedding at a venue Comeketo already partners with, that's a referral asset sitting unused. One line from Camila to the venue contact ("how's the [couple] booking shaping up on your end?") moves the deal sideways through a channel the couple trusts more than us. Free leverage, only works for a subset, but the subset is real.

3. **"72-hour line — anything past it gets a phone call, anything inside it gets a written redirect."**
Sharp threshold, no negotiation. Past 72 hours of silence, the cadence is broken and a written nudge won't fix it — pick up the phone. Inside 72, a specific written touch (referencing the tasting) is still in-window. Stops the team from sending day-9 emails that should be calls and day-2 calls that should be texts.

Bet's on #1 — the other two are tactical, but #1 reframes how the team *sees* the 14, which compounds past this batch.

text: Bucket the 14 pending tastings by objection shape (price / comparison / deprioritized) and run a tailored motion per bucket.

content: Let's talk about: FB/IG/Crisp Chat is tied for the top lead source but chat leads convert worse than webs…

title: FB/IG/Crisp Chat is tied for the top lead source but chat leads convert worse than webs…

content:
Yeah, this is the one I'd actually chase before the attribution swamp — it's a speed problem masquerading as a quality problem.

Three things worth separating before the team touches it:

1. **The notification lag is probably the whole story.** IG DM pings a phone in seconds. Crisp Chat routes through a web widget → email → whoever's logged in. If the speed-to-lead anchor (0.95, the heaviest one we've got) is firing late on Crisp specifically, that's not a "chat converts worse" problem, that's a "Crisp is structurally slower" problem. Pull median first-response time by source for the last 30 days. If Crisp's median is >5 min and IG/FB is <5, you've found it.

2. **Chat leads might also be earlier-stage humans.** Someone who DMs "hey do you do Brazilian BBQ for 80 people" on IG is more committed than someone who pops a Crisp bubble that says "questions?" on the website. Same source bucket, different intent depth. Worth checking whether the Crisp leads are asking *one* question and bouncing, vs. the IG ones who are starting a thread. If yes, the conversion gap isn't a rep problem — it's that Crisp is catching browsers, not buyers.

3. **Rep-level cut before any system change.** Before anyone touches Crisp's settings or the routing, see if one rep is eating most of the chat volume and underperforming on it. If Toni gets 70% of the Crisp pings and converts at half her IG rate, it's a rep-channel mismatch, not a channel problem. Cheap to check, expensive to skip.

My bet is #1 explains 60% of the gap and #2 explains the rest. #3 is the diagnostic that rules out the boring answer before we go reorganizing tooling.

What do you want to do — pull the median-response numbers, or go straight at Crisp's notification setup?

text: Pull median first-response time by lead source for last 30 days to test whether Crisp's notification lag explains the conversion gap.
